findings.title: Run listing uses a live dict view outside the store lock
findings.detail: Iterating a dict values view after releasing the lock can raise runtime errors or return inconsistent results when concurrent writes occur.

findings.title: Migration failures can leave runs stuck in migrating
findings.detail: The API transitions a run to migrating before verifying the executor import and before guarding the migration call, so failures can strand the run without a terminal status.

findings.title: Target crawl exports raw sample rows
findings.detail: Sample rows are copied verbatim into crawl output, which creates a direct path for PII and secret leakage into downstream persistence and review artifacts.

findings.title: Migration metrics assume graph nodes are objects instead of dicts
findings.detail: When graph nodes are stored as dictionaries, the current mapping count logic reports zero candidates and makes migration telemetry misleading.

findings.title: Orchestrator response parsing assumes a narrow OpenAI payload shape
findings.detail: The parser only checks a small set of response fields, so supported API variations or wrapped structured output can fail without a durable fallback path.

findings.title: Structured output parsing does not preserve context on JSON decode failures
findings.detail: Malformed or wrapped model output will bubble up as a raw JSON decode failure, which makes recurring contract issues harder to diagnose.

findings.title: Backend review targets are missing automated tests
findings.detail: No backend-focused test modules were found under the reviewed surfaces, so the Ralph loop can report recurring issues but cannot validate regressions automatically.

findings.title: Run status transitions wipe started_at for non-running states
findings.detail: Completed and failed runs lose their original start timestamp, which breaks duration tracking and weakens backend auditability.

findings.title: Uploaded filenames are written into storage paths without sanitization
findings.detail: A crafted filename can escape the intended upload area or create unstable local file paths because the store concatenates the raw filename into the saved path.

findings.title: Oracle preview logic trusts arbitrary file metadata paths
findings.detail: The crawler reads the first line of whatever path is present in file metadata without confining that path to the upload area.
